name: Green Gnocchi with Cream Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Fresh spinach; washed and stems removed
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the spinach with just the water that clings to it from cleaning. Cool, then squeeze as dry as possible and chop finely.
2. Sauté the spinach and onion in butter until soft. Transfer to a bowl and stir in ricotta, Parmesan, egg, nutmeg, salt, and pepper (mixing first in a processor if desired).
3. Stir in flour; the mixture will be slightly sticky. Shape into small balls no larger than 3/4 inch in diameter, re-flouring hands as needed to prevent sticking.
4. Dry the shaped gnocchi on a floured baking tray. For freezing: place on trays until firm, then transfer to plastic bags.
5. To cook, bring salted water to boil. Add gnocchi and return to boil. When they float, remove with a slotted spoon and place in a warmed bowl.
6. Add Cream Sauce (see below) and Parmesan cheese; gently toss to coat well. Serve immediately.
Cream Sauce:
4 tbsp Butter
1 cup Heavy cream
3/4 cup Parmesan; freshly grated
Simmer over medium heat until thickened, about 20 to 30 minutes.
